name: Chiang Mai Steaks
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the coconut milk: Bring 1 1/2 cups water to a boil in a small saucepan. Add 1 cup packed, flaked coconut and simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes. Process in a blender at high speed for 1 to 2 minutes or until thoroughly blended. Strain the mixture, discarding the coconut.
2. Dust beef tenderloin steaks with flour and salt. Shake off excess flour and reserve.
3. Heat butter and oil in a large heavy frying pan over medium heat until hot.
4. Add steaks to the pan and pan fry 6 to 8 minutes or to desired doneness, turning once.
5. Remove steaks and keep warm.
6. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add reserved flour to the pan and cook just until brown, stirring constantly.
7. Stir in peanut butter and curry powder until smooth.
8. Gradually add coconut milk and cook until the sauce comes to a boil and thickens, stirring constantly.
9. Return steaks to the pan and turn to coat with sauce.
10. Place steaks on a heated platter. Garnish with kiwi slices and parsley sprigs.
11. Serve steaks with sauce. Makes 4 servings.
